data.title: Blue Hat Interactive priced US$6.4M public offering of 32M Units at US$0.20 each, expecting gross proceeds of US$6.4M.
data.description: In Xiamen, China, on February 19, 2026, Blue Hat Interactive Entertainment Technology (NASDAQ: BHAT) revealed details about its underwritten public offering. The Offering consists of 32,000,000 Units priced at US$0.20 per Unit, leading to an expected gross income of about US$6.4 million for the Company, before accounting for underwriting deductions and other costs. Each Unit comprises one Ordinary Share valued at US$0.0000001 per share and one Warrant, with the latter being initially exercisable for one Ordinary Share.

data.title: Foundation for Drug-Free World distributes 1M booklets in Italy during Winter Olympics to combat drug abuse. #MilanoCortina2026
data.description: In Milan on February 19, 2026, the Foundation for a Drug-Free World achieved a significant milestone by distributing over 1,000,000 The Truth About Drugs booklets throughout Italy during the Milano Cortina 2026 Winter Olympics. This initiative aims to address drug abuse within society. Rather than opting for instant solutions through medication...

data.title: Bimergen Energy Corp priced public offering at $4/share, raising $13.6M, with warrants included. Underwriters may purchase more shares.
data.description: On February 19, 2026, in Newport Beach, CA, Bimergen Energy Corporation, a leading utility-scale battery energy storage systems asset owner, disclosed a public offering of common stock and Pre-Funded Warrants at $4.00 each, generating $13,600,000 prior to deductions. Each unit includes one accompanying warrant, exercisable at $5.00 for a period of five years. Furthermore, the underwriters have an option to acquire an additional 510,000 shares of common stock and/or warrants within 45 days to cover over-allotments. The company trades on NYSE American under the tickers BESS and BESSWS.

data.title: Global nonprofit 50/50 Women on Boards opens 2026 Summit with The Breakfast of Corporate Champions to advocate for gender diversity in business governance. Founded by Janice Ellig in 2011.
data.description: In Los Angeles on February 19, 2026, 50/50 Women on Boards, a prominent nonprofit organization focused on advancing gender equality in corporate governance, disclosed its management of The Breakfast of Corporate Champions. This event, initiated in 2011 by Janice Ellig, the current CEO, aims to promote diversity on corporate boards.

data.title: Just Women's Sports revives 'Between The Lines' with Lisa Leslie, sponsored by Intuit TurboTax. First guest: Kelsey Mitchell. LOS ANGELES, Feb. 19, 2026.
data.description: A renowned interview series, recognized for its honest discussions with top basketball players, is making a comeback with Kelsey Mitchell as the inaugural guest. The show, named Between the Lines, is produced by Just Women's Sports and is set to premiere on Feb. 19, 2026, in Los Angeles. The series has received accolades for its engaging conversations with prominent athletes, offering viewers an inside look at the lives and perspectives of basketball's biggest stars. Stay tuned for more insightful interviews and exciting moments as the series unfolds.

data.title: Negotiations resume among White House, banks, and crypto groups regarding stablecoin rewards in the digital asset market legislation.
data.description: Lawmakers are currently working on advancing legislation concerning digital-asset market structures, with stablecoin incentives being a prominent unresolved issue. The importance of stablecoin incentives is highlighted as legislators aim to address key aspects of the digital asset market. The specific details regarding the legislation and the exact incentives being discussed remain undisclosed, but it is a significant aspect with numbers indicating its relevance in shaping the future regulatory framework.

data.title: Abundia priced $20M registered direct offering of 5,934,718 common stock shares, to result in gross proceeds.
data.description: On Feb. 19, 2026, Abundia Global Impact Group, Inc. (NYSE: AGIG) revealed a new funding development. They have struck a deal with a fundamental institutional investor to sell 5,934,718 shares of common stock (or pre-funded warrants) in a registered direct offering. This initiative is anticipated to generate around $20 million in gross proceeds, with the closing scheduled for Feb. 23, 2026. The company, specializing in low-carbon energy solutions by converting biomass and plastics waste into valuable low-carbon fuels, aims to utilize this investment for further growth.
